mnemonics: PIP: Primitives are Passed by value, Objects are Passed by reference
keyPoints: Primitive arguments (int, double, boolean, etc.) are passed by value; a copy of the value is sent to the method.
Object references are passed by value; the method receives a copy of the reference pointing to the original object.
A method can change the state of an object passed to it (using the copied reference) but cannot reassign the original caller's reference to a new object.
commonMistakes: Believing that objects themselves are passed by reference (it's the reference that is passed by value).
Assuming that changing an object's state inside a method means Java is pass-by-reference for all types.
